tipRules.tip: Yamamoto pro Hite: The Zako was designed as a chatter-style bait trailer; its restrained action won't overpower the vibrating jig — most other soft plastic trailers exhibit too much action or aren't durable enough

Yamamoto pro Hite: The Zako rigged Texas-style replicates the subtle gliding of a tube; use it as a flipping bait around aquatic or flooded terrestrial vegetation during the spawn or whenever bass are in shallow-water haunts

knowledge.insight: Yamamoto pro Hite: The Zako was designed with restrained 'no-feel' action to mimic how bluegill, green sunfish, and gizzard shad hover and subtly swim; the V-shaped tail with an interior flange minimizes tail movement; a back groove facilitates easy rigging; non-salt-impregnated blend for increased durability
Yamamoto pro Hite convinced Colby to create a non-salt-impregnated plastic blend for the Zako for increased durability — can withstand multiple fish catches without tearing
